<!-- generated by tests/validator_reference_doc.rs; regenerate with `cargo test --test validator_reference_doc -- --ignored generate` -->

# T.88 validator reference

This file enumerates every `CheckId` produced by `jbig2::validator` together with the spec passage it cites. The catalog is the source of truth; this document is regenerated from it.

## How to add a new check

1. Pick the smallest spec clause that the check belongs to and add a new `struct` to the matching `src/validator/catalog/clause_*.rs` module (or create one if needed).
2. Implement `Check`. The `id()` must be a stable `T88-<clause>-NNN` identifier; the `cite()` must quote a verbatim passage from `vendor/T-REC-T.88-201808/spec/ITU-T_T_88__08_2018.md` (or the relevant Annex).
3. Add the new struct to the clause module's `pub fn checks()`.
4. Add a positive fixture to `tools/corpus-mint/main.rs` (under `synthetic_fixtures()`) that surfaces the new id.
5. Inline a `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` block beside the check with at least one positive and one negative case, mirroring `clause_7_4_2.rs`.
6. Run `cargo run --bin corpus-mint --features validator-corpus` and `cargo test --test validator_reference_doc -- --ignored generate` to keep this doc in sync.

## Corpus layout

`tests/validator-corpus/` holds the regression corpus. Sub-directories:

- `synthetic/<clause>-<short-name>/` — hand-crafted streams produced by `tools/corpus-mint`. Each fixture targets exactly one CheckId.
- `annex-h-bitflip/bucket-<severity>-<id>-<seg-type>/byte-NNNN-bit-N/` — exhaustive single-bit-flip sweep over the canonical Annex H stream.
- `mutated/annex-h/<schedule>/...` — deterministic, seed-driven mutation cases (bit-flip, byte-replace, dlen-perturb).
- `bugzilla/ghostscript-<bug-id>/` — third-party JBIG2 streams harvested from `bugs.ghostscript.com`. `expected.toml` uses `primary_check_id = "unknown"` until a maintainer hand-classifies the bug; the regression test only requires the validator to terminate without panicking on these fixtures.

## Catalog

| CheckId | Default severity | Spec clause | Citation |
|---|---|---|---|
| `T88-D.4-001` | Error | `Annex D.4` | The JBIG2 file header shall begin with the 8-octet ID string and the following file header flags byte. |
| `T88-D.4-002` | Error | `Annex D.4` | If the number of pages is unknown then the number-of-pages field shall not be present; otherwise it shall contain the number of pages in th… |
| `T88-7.2.1-001` | Error | `7.2.1` | Segment numbers shall be assigned in ascending order. |
| `T88-7.2.2-001` | Error | `7.2.2` | The segment type field shall contain one of the segment type values defined in Table 2. |
| `T88-7.2.3-001` | Error | `7.2.3` | Values 5 and 6 of the three-bit referred-to segment count field are reserved. |
| `T88-7.2.4-001` | Error | `7.2.4` | The retain flags shall contain one flag for each referred-to segment and one flag for the current segment. |
| `T88-7.2.7-001` | Error | `7.2.7` | The segment data length field shall give the number of octets in the segment data field. |
| `T88-7.2.7-002` | Error | `7.2.7` | The value 0xffffffff may be used only where the standard permits an unknown segment data length. |
| `T88-7.3.1-001` | Error | `7.3.1` | A referred-to segment number shall identify a segment in the current JBIG2 stream. |
| `T88-7.3.1-002` | Error | `7.3.1` | A segment shall not refer to a segment that follows it in the sequential organization. |
| `T88-7.3.2-001` | Error | `7.3.2` | A page-associated region segment shall be associated with a page for which page information is present. |
| `T88-7.3.2-002` | Error | `7.3.2` | Text region segments shall refer to the symbol dictionaries from which symbol IDs are decoded. |
| `T88-7.4.1-001` | Error | `7.4.1` | The region segment information field shall specify the width and height of the region bitmap. |
| `T88-7.4.1-002` | Error | `7.4.1` | Only the defined external combination operator and colour-extension bits are present in the region segment information flags. |
| `T88-7.4.2-001` | Error | `7.4.2` | The symbol dictionary segment flags field contains the SDHUFF, SDREFAGG, template, table selection, and reserved bits defined by Table 18. |
| `T88-7.4.2-002` | Error | `7.4.2` | SDNUMEXSYMS and SDNUMNEWSYMS shall describe the number of exported and newly decoded symbols in the dictionary. |
| `T88-7.4.2-009` | Error | `7.4.2` | When SDREFAGG is one, refinement aggregate instances shall refer only to symbols already available from the imported and previously decoded… |
| `T88-7.4.3-001` | Error | `7.4.3` | The text region segment flags and text region Huffman flags shall contain only the values defined for the text region segment. |
| `T88-7.4.3-002` | Error | `7.4.3` | SBNUMINSTANCES specifies the number of symbol instances in the text region. |
| `T88-7.4.4-001` | Error | `7.4.4` | The pattern dictionary segment shall define HDMMR, HDTEMPLATE, HDPW, HDPH, and GRAYMAX in the segment data header. |
| `T88-7.4.5-001` | Error | `7.4.5` | The halftone region segment data header shall contain HMMR, HTEMPLATE, HENABLESKIP, HCOMBOP, HDEFPIXEL, and grid parameters. |
| `T88-7.4.6-001` | Error | `7.4.6` | The generic region segment flags field shall define MMR, GBTEMPLATE, TPGDON, EXTTEMPLATE, and reserved bits. |
| `T88-7.4.7-001` | Error | `7.4.7` | The generic refinement region segment flags field shall contain GRTEMPLATE, TPGRON, and reserved bits. |
| `T88-7.4.7-002` | Error | `7.4.7` | Generic refinement regions shall refer to a bitmap source segment to be refined. |
| `T88-7.4.8-001` | Error | `7.4.8` | The page information segment shall specify page dimensions, resolution, page flags, and striping information. |
| `T88-7.4.9-001` | Error | `7.4.9` | An end-of-page segment shall indicate the end of a page. |
| `T88-7.4.11-001` | Error | `7.4.11` | The end-of-file segment shall occur after all other segments in the JBIG2 file. |
| `T88-7.4.12-001` | Error | `7.4.12` | The profiles segment shall list the profiles to which the coded representation conforms. |
| `T88-7.4.13-001` | Error | `7.4.13` | A tables segment shall contain a well-formed table definition used by later Huffman-coded segments. |
| `T88-7.4.14-001` | Error | `7.4.14` | The colour palette segment shall describe the number and values of colours used by colour extension regions. |
| `T88-7.4.15-001` | Error | `7.4.15` | The extension segment shall begin with an extension type field and extension flags. |
| `T88-E.1.3-001` | Error | `Annex E.1.3` | The arithmetic-coded byte stream shall be terminated and byte aligned as specified for the MQ coder. |
| `T88-A-001` | Error | `Annex A` | Integer arithmetic coding procedures shall decode integers within the range required by the associated coding context. |
| `T88-6.2.5.3-001` | Error | `6.2.5.3` | Adaptive template pixel positions shall be present and legal for the selected generic-region template. |

## Lenses

| Lens | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `Lens::StrictT88` | Default. Reports every catalog check at its declared severity. |
| `Lens::Jbig2decInterop` | Re-weights checks that Artifex `jbig2dec` ignores in practice. |
| `Lens::ItuT88Interop` | Re-weights checks that the ITU-T T.88 reference codec accepts. |
| `Lens::ImageioInterop` | Re-weights checks that PDFBox `jbig2-imageio` ignores. |

## Severity semantics

- `Error`  - strict T.88 violation; renders as `BAD(N)` in matrix cells.
- `Warning` - non-fatal interop concern; renders as `WARN(N)`.
- `Info`   - advisory note (rare); does not affect verdicts.
